meta.name: Grit-S Short Grit Scale
meta.description: The Grit-S is an 8-item measure of trait-level perseverance and passion for long-term goals. It includes two subscales: Consistency of Interest and Perseverance of Effort.
meta.author: Duckworth & Quinn (2009)
meta.reference: Duckworth AL, Quinn PD. Development and validation of the Short Grit Scale (Grit-S). J Pers Assess. 2009;91(2):166-174.
meta.instruction: Please respond to each item using the scale below. Be honest; there are no right or wrong answers.

questions.text: New ideas and projects sometimes distract me from previous ones
I have been obsessed with a certain idea or project for a short time but later lost interest
I often set a goal but later choose to pursue a different one
I have difficulty maintaining my focus on projects that take more than a few months to complete

ranges.label: Low Grit
ranges.description: Your grit score is low, suggesting substantial difficulty sustaining effort and interest toward long-term goals. You may frequently shift between projects or lose motivation when progress feels slow.
suggestions: Begin by setting smaller, clearly defined goals that can serve as achievable stepping stones toward larger objectives
Practice committing to one project or goal at a time before taking on additional pursuits
Spend time reflecting on what truly matters to you in the long term to identify goals worth persevering toward
Consider working with a coach or mentor who can provide accountability and guidance on maintaining focus

ranges.label: Below Average Grit
ranges.description: Your grit is below average, indicating that you may sometimes struggle to maintain consistent focus on long-term goals. Interest in new pursuits may occasionally divert attention from existing commitments.
suggestions: Identify your core passions and align your goals with activities that genuinely inspire sustained interest
Build daily habits and routines that support consistency, such as dedicated work blocks and progress tracking
Track your progress visually using journals or apps to maintain motivation and recognize incremental gains
Learn to view setbacks as valuable learning opportunities rather than reasons to abandon your goals

ranges.label: Above Average Grit
ranges.description: Your grit is above average, indicating that you generally maintain sustained effort and interest in your long-term goals. You demonstrate a healthy balance between persistence and adaptability in pursuing objectives.
suggestions: Continue cultivating your perseverance by setting progressively more challenging goals for yourself
Share your strategies for persistence and follow-through with peers or colleagues who may benefit from them
Challenge yourself with increasingly ambitious long-term projects that stretch your capabilities

ranges.label: High Grit
ranges.description: Your grit score is high, demonstrating exceptional perseverance and sustained passion for your long-term goals. You consistently follow through on commitments and maintain focus even when facing significant obstacles.
suggestions: Continue your excellent persistence habits while remaining mindful of opportunities to rest and recharge
Ensure you also allow appropriate flexibility when circumstances require adjusting your goals or strategies
Use your grit as a strength to inspire and motivate others in collaborative and leadership settings
